tool.name: EnzyWizard Dock
tool.description: Perform molecular docking of one or multiple substrates with a cleaned protein structure
tool.command: ln -s '$input_path' input.cif && mkdir -p substrate_dir && unzip -q '$substrate_zip' -d substrate_dir && enzywizard-dock -i input.cif -s '$substrate_names' -d substrate_dir -o ./ --max_docking_attempt_num '$max_docking_attempt_num' $no_early_stop --exhaustiveness '$exhaustiveness' --cpu "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" --min_rad '$min_rad' --max_rad '$max_rad' --min_volume '$min_volume' #if $catalytic_residue: --catalytic_residue '$catalytic_residue' #end if #if $catalytic_site_coord: --catalytic_site_coord '$catalytic_site_coord' #end if #if $box_size: --box_size '$box_size' #end if

expand.name: substrate_zip
expand.label: Substrate directory (zip)
expand.help: Zip archive containing substrate SDF files.

tool.help: Perform molecular docking of one or multiple substrates with a cleaned protein structure. Directory inputs are represented as zip datasets and unpacked explicitly inside this wrapper command in the Galaxy job working directory. Outputs are exposed as discovered dataset collections.
